_verification_note: Canonical index of KS 1700:2018 deviations from BS 7671:2018+A2 baseline. Each deviation cross-references the per-clause file where the deviation content lives. Verify against published KS 1700:2018 PDF.
_purpose: Single source of truth for engineers asking 'where does KS 1700 differ from BS 7671?'. Each row documents one deviation with rationale + engineering impact + applicable clauses.

deviations.summary: 30mA RCD additional protection mandatory on all socket circuits ≤32A regardless of Zs compliance
deviations.diverges_from: BS 7671:2018 pre-AMD2 socket-RCD selective rule (BS 7671 AMD 2 (2022) brought BS into alignment)
deviations.rationale: Kenya climate (humidity, particularly coastal) plus end-user risk profile justify universal socket-RCD; aligned with BS AMD 2 (2022) but applied from initial KS publication in 2018
deviations.engineering_impact: Every Kenya design with socket circuits ≤32A must include 30mA RCD; engineers should NOT omit RCD even where Zs compliance is achieved

deviations.summary: Ambient design temperature baselines per Kenya climate zone (highland 25°C indoor / coastal 35°C / inland tropical 30°C / arid northern 35°C)
deviations.rationale: Coastal humidity + inland highland diurnal range + arid northern climate require zone-specific baselines for accurate cable ampacity design
deviations.engineering_impact: Cable ampacity derating coefficient Ca applied per zone; effective cable size larger than BS-baseline calculation in coastal + arid zones; smaller in highland zone

deviations.summary: KS 1700:2018 has no native §722 EV charging clause; KS Annex E §VIII directs to IEC 60364-7-722:2018
deviations.diverges_from: BS 7671:2018+A2 Part 7 §722 (added by BS AMD 2 in 2022)
deviations.rationale: KS 1700:2018 first edition predates BS 7671 AMD 2 (2022); EV charging is in scope for the next KS revision (estimated 2024 cycle)
deviations.engineering_impact: EV installations in Kenya apply IEC 60364-7-722 directly + KS 1700 §411/§544/§415 baseline; future KS revision will incorporate native §722

deviations.summary: Outdoor installations in Kenya coastal zone (within 10km of Indian Ocean) and lakeshore zones require IP65 minimum
deviations.diverges_from: BS 7671 §522 silent on Kenya-specific coastal humidity
deviations.rationale: High humidity + salt-laden air on Kenya coast (Mombasa, Kilifi, Kwale, Lamu) accelerate corrosion of enclosures below IP65
deviations.engineering_impact: Outdoor equipment in coastal counties specified at IP65 minimum; informally enforced by EPRA inspections

deviations.summary: All consumer-side electrical equipment sold in Kenya must carry KEBS certification mark
deviations.diverges_from: BS 7671 silent on consumer-protection certification mandate
deviations.rationale: Kenyan Standards Act Cap 496 mandates KEBS Mark for sale of regulated products; consumer protection
deviations.engineering_impact: Engineers should specify KEBS-certified equipment; reject deliveries of unmarked devices

deviations.summary: Mandatory periodic inspection of installations: ≤5 years commercial/industrial, ≤10 years domestic, by EPRA-registered electrical contractor
deviations.diverges_from: BS 7671 recommends periodic inspection but no statutory mandate (UK BS 7671 is consumer-protection guidance only)
deviations.rationale: Kenya Electricity Act provides statutory mandate for periodic inspection regime; EPRA enforces
deviations.engineering_impact: Initial verification certificate + scheduled periodic inspection required; engineer/contractor must be EPRA-registered
